content: Hi, I'm back from my sabbatical and I'm feeling refreshed and ready to learn some new skills. I've been an Interior Designer for commercial spaces for over 10 years, but I want to expand my skill set.
Welcome back! It's great that you're feeling refreshed and ready to learn. What specific skills are you interested in acquiring?

I've been thinking about learning more about sustainable design and maybe even getting certified in LEED. I've also been interested in woodworking and home brewing beer, but I'm not sure how to fit those into my schedule.
Sustainable design and LEED certification are great skills to have in the industry. Woodworking and home brewing beer sound like fun hobbies - do you have any experience with them already?

I've dabbled in woodworking, but I'd love to take some classes to improve my skills. And with home brewing beer, I've been experimenting with different recipes, but I'd love to learn more about the science behind it. I've been inspired by some of the breweries I visited during my travels in Eastern Europe.
There are many great classes and workshops available for woodworking and home brewing beer. Have you considered taking any online courses or attending local workshops?

I've been looking into some online courses, but I'm not sure which ones to choose. Do you have any recommendations? I've also been thinking about starting a travel blog to document my experiences and share my knowledge of different cultures and design styles.
There are many great online courses available. Can you tell me more about your experience with interior design and what specific areas you're interested in learning more about?

I've been working in commercial interior design for over 10 years, and I've been focusing on creating sustainable and functional spaces. But I feel like I need to expand my skill set to include more technical skills, like CAD design and project management. My colleague, Rachel, has been a great resource, but I want to learn more.
CAD design and project management are great skills to have in the industry. Have you considered joining online communities or forums to connect with other designers and learn from their experiences?

Yeah, I've been part of a few online communities, including the Interior Designers group on LinkedIn. But I'd love to find more resources, maybe something that focuses on sustainable design and LEED certification. I've been thinking about attending the Greenbuild conference in November.

The Greenbuild conference is a great resource for learning about sustainable design and LEED certification. I can provide you with some information on how to get the most out of the conference. In the meantime, can you tell me more about your travel blog idea? What kind of content are you planning to create?

I'm thinking about creating a blog that focuses on sustainable design and travel, and maybe even including some recipes and cooking tips from my experiences with Thai food. I've been inspired by some of the food bloggers I've met during my travels.

That sounds like a great concept. I can help you brainstorm some ideas for your blog. What kind of platform are you planning to use for your blog, and do you have any experience with writing and photography?

I've been thinking about using WordPress, and I've got some experience with writing and photography from my travel blogging days. But I'm not sure about the technical side of things - do you have any recommendations for plugins and themes? I'm hoping to launch the blog by the end of the year, maybe around December 20th.

WordPress is a great platform for blogging. I can recommend some popular plugins and themes for you to consider. December 20th is a great target date for launching your blog - I can help you create a plan to make it happen.
I'd love to get some feedback on my blog idea and maybe even get some suggestions for monetization strategies. I've been thinking about offering consulting services or creating online courses.

I'd be happy to help you brainstorm some ideas for monetization strategies. Consulting services and online courses are both great options - what kind of expertise do you think you could offer, and what kind of courses would you be interested in creating?
